text: Urgent: We just became aware that our contract manufacturer changed a component supplier for the CPAP blower motor without notifying us. This is a supplier change that may require a new 510(k) supplement or at minimum a regulatory assessment. Please advise on next steps.
label: regulatory

text: Need help understanding the UDI labeling requirements for our new pulse oximeter accessory. Is it considered a device or an accessory under 21 CFR 801? This impacts how we register it in GUDID.
label: regulatory

text: Created a branch for the ADC sampling rate improvement on the pulse oximeter. Moved from 100Hz to 500Hz sampling. Initial results look promising — signal quality is noticeably better, especially at low perfusion. PR is up for review: PR-4821.
label: engineering

text: Architecture decision needed: For the next-gen patient data gateway, should we go with MQTT or AMQP for device-to-cloud messaging? MQTT is lighter weight and better for our constrained devices, but AMQP gives us better message reliability guarantees. Let's discuss at the next architecture sync.
label: engineering

text: I need access to the FDA MAUDE database export tool. Trying to pull adverse event reports for predicate devices as part of our 510(k) submission literature review. Who has the login credentials?
label: regulatory

text: Heads up — I'm merging the feature branch for HL7 FHIR integration into develop today. It touches the data model layer pretty heavily so expect some merge conflicts if you have outstanding branches against the patient record module. Ping me if you need help resolving anything.
label: engineering

text: Does anyone know if we have a corporate discount for FedEx/UPS shipping for personal use, or is that only for business shipments?
label: general

text: Heads up: new FDA guidance document just dropped on cybersecurity for medical devices. It's now a refuse-to-accept criterion for premarket submissions. We need to review our cybersecurity documentation for the CardioLink wireless monitor and determine if any gaps exist before we file.
label: regulatory

text: Requesting a new Git repository for the SpO2 module rewrite. Repo name: spo2-signal-processing-v2. Need it under the embedded-devices org with the standard branch protection rules and CI/CD hooks.
label: engineering

text: How do I submit an expense report for the clinical site visit I did last week in Minneapolis? I have receipts for airfare, hotel, rental car, and meals. First time doing this so any guidance appreciated.
label: general

text: During our mock FDA inspection last Thursday, we identified a gap: our CAPA system doesn't clearly link corrective actions back to the specific regulatory requirement that was violated. This is a 483 waiting to happen. Requesting a process improvement initiative.
label: regulatory

text: Jenkins pipeline for the respiratory therapy device is broken after the migration to the new build agents. The ARM cross-compiler toolchain isn't installed on the new nodes. Can DevOps add the gcc-arm-none-eabi-10.3 package to the agent Docker image?
label: engineering

text: Can we get the visitor Wi-Fi password updated and posted in the lobby? We have auditors, clinical partners, and suppliers visiting regularly and the front desk keeps getting overwhelmed with requests. Maybe a small sign at reception?
label: general

text: I'd like to sign up for the company's tuition reimbursement program. I'm looking at a graduate certificate in biomedical engineering from Johns Hopkins. It's directly relevant to my role on the device design team. What's the process and deadline for the spring semester?
label: general

text: This is probably a silly question but — who do I talk to about getting a company polo or jacket? I have a few customer site visits coming up and want to look professional. I saw other people wearing branded gear but I never got any.
label: general

text: Software verification testing for the wireless firmware update feature is complete. 87 of 87 test cases passed. Summary report is drafted and uploaded to the DHF. Requesting QA manager approval to close out this verification activity.
label: qa

text: Need to refactor the alarm prioritization logic in the patient monitor codebase. Right now we're using a simple priority queue but we need to handle simultaneous high-priority alarms per IEC 60601-1-8. I'm proposing we move to a weighted scoring system. Setting up a design review for Thursday — please review the RFC in Confluence before then.
label: engineering

text: The Design History File for the RespiCare Pro is missing the risk management report. ISO 14971 requires this to be part of the DHF. Can we get the risk team to finalize and upload to the document control system ASAP? We have a notified body audit in 3 weeks.
label: regulatory

text: We should migrate the device configuration service from Python 2 (yes, it's still on Python 2) to Python 3. This is a ticking time bomb. Several dependencies are already EOL. I can take this on next sprint if we can get it prioritized.
label: engineering

text: Test case TC-4021 for the new CPAP pressure ramp algorithm needs to be updated. The acceptance criteria changed after the last design review — max ramp time was reduced from 45 min to 30 min. Can someone from the QA team revise and get it back into the queue for the next verification cycle?
label: qa

text: The automated test suite for the patient compliance reporting module failed overnight. 14 out of 203 test cases failed, mostly related to date/time formatting edge cases around daylight saving time transitions. Looks like a real issue, not flaky tests. Assigning to QA for investigation and defect logging.
label: qa

text: Can someone let me know how to reserve the large conference room on the 3rd floor? The Outlook room finder isn't showing it as an option anymore.
label: general

text: The software validation documentation for our patient data management system needs to comply with IEC 62304. Current documentation only references our internal software development SOP but doesn't map to the standard's requirements. Regulatory needs engineering to close this gap before our submission.
label: regulatory

text: We need to define acceptance criteria for the usability validation of the new touchscreen interface on the bedside cardiac monitor. The human factors team provided their report last week. QA should translate those findings into measurable pass/fail criteria for the summative evaluation. Meeting to discuss Thursday at 2pm.
label: qa

text: Hi, I'm a new hire starting on the respiratory therapy devices team and my badge doesn't give me access to Building C. Who do I contact to get this updated? I need access by Monday for onboarding.
label: general

text: We have a memory leak in the bedside monitor application. After approximately 72 hours of continuous runtime, heap usage climbs to 94% and the UI starts dropping frames. I've narrowed it down to the waveform rendering module — looks like ECG trace buffers aren't being freed when switching between patient views. Marking this as P1 since it affects clinical use.
label: engineering

text: Bug report: Bluetooth Low Energy pairing between the CPAP device and the companion mobile app fails intermittently on Android 14 devices. Error code 133 (GATT_ERROR) appears in the logs. Seems related to the new Android BLE stack changes. Reproducible about 30% of the time on Pixel 8.
label: engineering

text: Need to update my direct deposit information. Switching banks. Is this something I do through Workday or do I need to fill out a paper form with HR?
label: general

text: Hi team, just a heads up — Health Canada sent a request for our Medical Device License amendment. They want the updated IFU translated into French. Can we get technical writing and translation services engaged? Regulatory will handle the submission once docs are ready.
label: regulatory

text: We need to file an MDR (Medical Device Report) with the FDA. A patient using our cardiac monitor reported that the device failed to alarm during a detected arrhythmia event. The complaint investigation confirmed a software anomaly. This meets the serious injury reporting criteria under 21 CFR 803.50 and we have a 30-day reporting window. Regulatory is drafting the report now — need engineering's root cause analysis by EOD Wednesday.
label: regulatory

text: Stack overflow crash in the alarm processing thread. Core dump shows recursive call in the escalation handler when two alarms trigger within the same millisecond. Adding a guard clause and increasing the thread stack size from 4KB to 8KB as an immediate fix, but we should refactor the escalation logic to be iterative.
label: engineering

text: Audit trail question — during the last internal audit, we noticed that several DHF documents for the cardiac monitor v2.3 were approved out of sequence. Design verification was signed off before the design input document was finalized. How do we remediate this?
label: regulatory

text: Firmware OTA update mechanism is not properly validating the digital signature on update packages. If the signature check fails, the device should reject the update and stay on the current version, but right now it's proceeding with the install anyway. This is a critical security issue.
label: engineering

text: Need help setting up the hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) test environment for the new ventilator controller board. The JTAG debugger isn't recognizing the new rev C PCB. Might be a pin mapping issue with the debug header.
label: engineering

text: The kitchen on the 2nd floor has had no paper towels or dish soap for over a week now. Could we please get those restocked? Also the ice machine seems to be leaking water onto the floor which is a slip hazard.
label: general

text: Can someone confirm whether our cardiac telemetry monitor falls under Class II or Class III? Marketing wants to expand the indications for use to include pediatric patients and I need to know the regulatory pathway implications before we commit.
label: regulatory

text: Do we have a current FDA Establishment Registration? I believe it was due for annual renewal on October 1st. Also need to verify all device listings are up to date including the new SKUs we launched in August.
label: regulatory

text: Process validation question: For the incoming QC inspection of the CPAP tubing assemblies, are we sampling per ANSI/ASQ Z1.4 Level II normal inspection, or did we switch to tightened inspection after the last supplier nonconformance? I want to make sure the sampling plan in our QA work instruction is current before we receive the next shipment.
label: qa

text: Hey, the CPAP blower motor PID control loop is oscillating at low pressure settings (4-6 cmH2O). I think the derivative gain is too aggressive. Anyone have time to pair on tuning this today?
label: engineering

text: Post-market surveillance report for Q3 is due to regulatory by end of month. We have 12 complaint investigations that need to be assessed for reportability under MDR and FDA MDR (21 CFR 803). Can the complaints team prioritize closure of the open investigations?
label: regulatory

text: Can we get SonarQube added to the CI pipeline for the cloud dashboard project? We need static analysis running on every PR. The codebase has grown a lot and we're starting to accumulate tech debt in the data ingestion service.
label: engineering

text: Is anyone else having trouble accessing the TestRail instance? I keep getting a 502 error when trying to log results for the CPAP data sync module. Need this resolved today — we have a verification milestone due Friday.
label: qa

text: Requesting regulatory review of proposed marketing claims for the BreathEasy CPAP. Sales wants to say "clinically proven to improve sleep quality by 40%" but I don't think we have the clinical data to support this and it could be considered misbranding under the FD&C Act.
label: regulatory

text: Requesting approval for travel to the MedTech World conference in Orlando, Nov 12-14. I'd like to attend sessions on connected health devices and remote patient monitoring trends. Flight + hotel estimated around $1,800. Manager has verbally approved, just need it in the system.
label: general

text: is the eCopy format still required for 510k submissions or did FDA switch to eSTAR for everything now? I'm prepping the submission for CardioSense Mini and want to make sure we use the right format.
label: regulatory

text: Traceability matrix for the respiratory rate monitoring feature is incomplete. We have 6 requirements in DOORS that don't map to any test cases yet. These were added in the last change order (ECO-3341). QA needs to close this gap before we can submit the verification summary.
label: qa

text: We need to update the 510(k) submission package for the new CPAP mask design. The predicate device comparison table is still referencing the old K-number. Can someone from regulatory affairs review and correct before we submit to FDA next week?
label: regulatory

text: Deployed the therapy compliance reporting microservice to staging. Seeing 500 errors on the /api/v2/sessions endpoint when the date range exceeds 90 days. The database query is timing out — need to add pagination or optimize the query. Postgres EXPLAIN output attached.
label: engineering

text: Hey — quick question. Are we off on the Friday after Thanksgiving this year or is that a floating holiday we need to use PTO for? I saw conflicting info in two different emails.
label: general

text: The projector in the Pulmonary conference room is broken again. Display flickers and then goes black after about 2 minutes. Can facilities take a look?
label: general

text: Opened defect DEF-0901. During environmental testing at 40°C/75% RH, the cardiac monitor's battery gauge reported 100% charge despite the battery being at approximately 60% actual capacity. This could be a safety concern if clinicians rely on the gauge during patient transport. Flagging for risk assessment.
label: qa

text: The nightly build for the CardioSync firmware v3.2.1 failed again. Looks like the linker is choking on the new HAL driver we pulled in for the pressure sensor module. Can someone from the embedded team take a look? Build log is attached.
label: engineering

text: Is there a company shuttle or carpool program for the new manufacturing facility out on Route 9? My commute just doubled since our team relocated and I know several others are in the same boat.
label: general

text: Lost my badge somewhere in Building A today. Can I get a temporary badge issued while I wait for the replacement? I need to be able to get into the office tomorrow morning. My employee ID is 20847.
label: general

text: We're planning a lunch-and-learn for the cardiac monitoring division next Thursday — topic is 'Understanding Our Customers: A Day in the Life of a Sleep Lab Technician.' Need to order catering for approximately 35 people. Any preferred vendors we have on file? Budget is around $500.
label: general

text: Per our CAPA-2024-009 corrective action, we need to add boundary condition test cases for all alarm threshold settings across the cardiac monitor product line. This involves approximately 40 new test cases. I've drafted the first 15 — can Sarah or Miguel pick up the remainder? Test case IDs reserved in TestRail under the CAPA Verification suite.
label: qa

text: Need regulatory sign-off on the labeling proofs for the SleepRight 200 before we send to print. Specifically need confirmation that the Rx only symbol, UDI barcode, and the updated warnings section meet 21 CFR 801 requirements.
label: regulatory

text: My ergonomic assessment was completed three weeks ago and the evaluator recommended a sit-stand desk and a different keyboard tray. I haven't heard anything since. Can someone check on the status of the equipment order? I'm having recurring wrist pain.
label: general

text: Requesting a formal review of the IQ/OQ/PQ protocols for the new manufacturing line pressure sensor calibration station. We need these signed off before we can proceed with the process validation. Draft documents are in the QA SharePoint folder under Line 4 Validation.
label: qa

text: During exploratory testing of the patient data export feature on the ResMed-compatible SD card module, I noticed that sessions shorter than 5 minutes are being recorded with a timestamp of 00:00:00 instead of the actual start time. Not sure if this is by design or a defect. Can QA confirm expected behavior against the SRS?
label: qa

text: Defect DEF-0892: The cardiac monitor's trending graph displays an incorrect scale when switching from 4-hour to 24-hour view. Y-axis labels overlap and some data points are clipped. Reproducible on firmware 3.2.1-rc2. Severity: Medium. Attaching screenshots.
label: qa

text: Just a heads up — I found a discrepancy between the test procedure document (TP-1120 Rev C) and what's actually scripted in the automated test framework for the CPAP auto-titration mode. The document says to test pressures from 4-20 cmH2O in 0.5 increments, but the script only tests 1.0 increments. Which one is correct?
label: qa

text: Hey, can someone check if the regression suite for the humidifier module was run after last Friday's firmware merge? I don't see results posted in TestRail.
label: qa

text: Docker Compose setup for local development of the patient portal is completely broken after the Redis upgrade. The health check is failing because the Redis container now requires authentication by default. Updated docker-compose.yml is in my branch — can someone verify it works on Mac as well? I only tested on Linux.
label: engineering

text: The HVAC in the clean room staging area (Room 214) has been running way too cold — multiple people on my team have complained. Thermostat shows 62°F. We normally keep it around 70. This isn't the controlled manufacturing floor, just the adjacent office/staging space, so it shouldn't be tied to environmental controls. Can facilities check?
label: general

text: Defect report: The CPAP device's compliance data occasionally shows therapy hours as negative values (-0.3 hours) when the patient removes the mask within the first 2 minutes of a session. Found during system-level testing with simulated patient profiles. Priority: High.
label: qa

text: The electronic signature system in our document control tool may not be compliant with 21 CFR Part 11. Specifically, I'm not sure we have adequate controls around signature manifestation meaning — it doesn't display the reason for signing (e.g., authored, reviewed, approved). This needs to be fixed before our next FDA inspection.
label: regulatory

text: Test execution for the cardiac monitor's ECG signal quality assessment module is blocked. The test fixture that simulates arrhythmia waveforms is malfunctioning — it's outputting a flat signal regardless of the selected pattern. Submitted a maintenance request but need an ETA so I can update the test schedule.
label: qa

text: The real-time clock (RTC) on the cardiac event recorder drifts about 2 seconds per day. Over a 30-day recording period that's a full minute of drift which is unacceptable for correlating cardiac events with timestamps. We need to implement NTP sync when the device is docked, or switch to a more accurate RTC crystal.
label: engineering

text: The SPI bus communication between the main processor and the CO2 sensor is timing out under heavy load. Need to bump the DMA buffer size or increase the clock divider. Going to prototype both solutions and benchmark.
label: engineering

text: The watchdog timer on the respiratory monitor is triggering unexpected resets during the power-on self-test sequence. The POST takes about 8 seconds on the new hardware but the WDT is configured for a 5-second timeout. Simple fix — just need to extend the timeout or add a WDT kick during POST.
label: engineering

text: Validation protocol VP-2024-017 for the mask fit detection feature is ready for peer review. It covers all acceptance criteria from the design input document and includes boundary value tests for the pressure differential thresholds. Please review and comment by EOD Thursday.
label: qa

text: Submitting a request for a second monitor for my workstation. I'm working across multiple design documents and the clinical data spreadsheets simultaneously and a single screen is really limiting my productivity. My manager, David Chen, has approved this.
label: general

text: Hey, quick one: do we need a new 510(k) for the updated humidifier chamber or can we file a letter-to-file? The material changed from polycarbonate to polypropylene but form/fit/function is identical.
label: regulatory

text: We found a defect during validation of the cardiac monitor alarm system. When SpO2 drops below 88% simultaneously with a heart rate spike above 150 bpm, the audible alarm triggers but the visual indicator on the bedside display doesn't illuminate. Logging as a critical defect — need triage ASAP.
label: qa

text: Reminder: the quarterly calibration of all test equipment in the QA lab is due by March 15. This includes the pressure manometers, flow analyzers, and electrical safety testers. Please check your bench equipment and flag anything that's overdue.
label: qa

text: Hi team, just a heads up that the VP of Operations all-hands meeting originally scheduled for Wednesday at 2pm has been moved to Thursday at 10am. Same room (Auditorium A). Please update your calendars. Agenda will be sent out tomorrow.
label: general

text: Got a question from our distributor in Japan about PMDA registration. They say we need a QMS audit under MHLW Ordinance 169 before they can file for Shonin approval. Can our regulatory team confirm requirements and timeline? This is blocking the Asia-Pacific launch.
label: regulatory

text: Can we get a risk-based assessment done on which regression tests to run for the v8.1 hotfix? It was a very narrow change (only the alarm volume lookup table), and running the full 900-case suite will take 3 days. I think a targeted subset is justified but need QA sign-off.
label: qa

text: The LCD display driver for the new humidifier control panel is rendering text with incorrect character encoding. UTF-8 special characters (like the degree symbol °) are showing up as garbage. Probably a font table issue in the display library.
label: engineering

text: Our team is growing and we're running out of space in our current area on the 4th floor. We're adding 3 new hires over the next 6 weeks (2 on the CPAP accessories line, 1 program coordinator). Is there any available workspace nearby or do we need to look at a different floor? Would like to keep the team co-located if possible.
label: general

text: Question about our predicate device for the 510(k) — the predicate we selected (K162345) has been recalled. Does this affect our submission strategy? Should we identify an alternative predicate or can we still reference a recalled device?
label: regulatory

text: I need to order new business cards. My title changed from Associate Product Manager to Product Manager as of last month. Can someone send me the request form or link?
label: general

text: I'm implementing the new data encryption module for patient health data stored on the device's SD card. Going with AES-256-GCM. Do we have an approved cryptographic library for our embedded platform, or do I need to evaluate options? Want to make sure we pick something that won't cause issues down the line with certification.
label: engineering

text: Hi team, I need to schedule the dry-run for the OQ protocol on the new packaging sealer before the official validation execution next week. Is the equipment available Tuesday afternoon? Also need a QA witness present.
label: qa

text: Just wanted to flag that the first aid kit in the break room near the device assembly area is almost empty. We used the last few bandages and antiseptic wipes last week after a minor incident. Should be restocked per OSHA requirements. Thanks!
label: general

text: Can we get access to the AWS IoT Core dev environment for the new device fleet management feature? I need to test MQTT topic routing and device shadow updates. My IAM user is jchen@meddevicecorp.com. Also going to need permissions to create Thing Groups for the prototype.
label: engineering

text: API versioning question: The mobile app team wants to deprecate v1 of the device data API. We still have ~12% of users on older app versions hitting v1. What's our policy on API sunset timelines? Do we need to coordinate with product on a forced update?
label: engineering

text: Quick question — are we using FreeRTOS or ThreadX for the new cardiac monitor platform? I need to know before I start writing the task scheduler for the sensor fusion module.
label: engineering

text: FDA just sent us an Additional Information request on our 510(k) for the SleepAssist 400 series. They want more biocompatibility data on the silicone cushion and a clarified intended use statement. We have 180 days to respond. Setting up a cross-functional team to address — regulatory, R&D, and toxicology need to be involved.
label: regulatory

text: Closing defect DEF-0847 as verified. Retested the fix for the CPAP therapy session summary rounding error on build 7.4.2.118. All five related test cases pass. Moving to closed status in Jira.
label: qa

text: Need to add 12 new test cases for the updated leak detection algorithm in the CPAP blower unit. Requirements were finalized yesterday in DOORS. Who's available to write these up this sprint?
label: qa

text: Annual report for our PMA device (CardioRhythm Implantable Monitor) is due January 1st. I need the following from each department: manufacturing changes summary, updated failure analysis data, complaint trending, and any labeling changes made during the reporting period.
label: regulatory

text: Reminder: all design change orders for released products must go through the regulatory impact assessment process before implementation. I've seen two ECOs this month that bypassed this step. Please review SOP-REG-042.
label: regulatory

text: We're seeing intermittent failures in the power-on self-test (POST) sequence on about 1 in 50 cardiac monitor units coming off the line. QA needs to determine if this is a hardware lot issue or a software timing problem. Can we set up a dedicated test bench to reproduce?
label: qa

text: Parking lot B has had a huge pothole near the entrance for weeks. Almost blew a tire this morning. Can we get this reported to whoever manages the lot?
label: general

text: Requesting a nameplate for my cubicle. I moved to the Patient Safety team two months ago and still have the old occupant's name on the wall.
label: general

text: Where do I find the updated employee handbook? The link on the intranet is giving me a 404 error.
label: general

text: Quick question — do we have a standard template for software verification test reports that aligns with IEC 62304? I'm writing up the results for the Bluetooth connectivity module and want to make sure I'm using the right format.
label: qa

text: We received a CE mark renewal notice from our EU Authorized Representative. The current certificate expires in 90 days. Need to compile the updated technical documentation per MDR 2017/745. Who owns the clinical evaluation report update?
label: regulatory
